key_insight: Scanning 3DXRD combined with crystal plasticity modeling reveals how rapid cyclic thermomechanical loading creates grain-resolved plastic deformation hotspots in Cu metallization.
engineering_relevance: The results help identify where degradation may initiate in power semiconductor metallization, supporting more reliable lifetime assessment and design of Cu interconnect layers.
method: Measured grain/subgrain deformation with scanning 3D X-ray diffraction (3DXRD).
Simulated rapid cyclic heating using thermomechanical crystal plasticity models.
Compared experiment and simulation to track degradation hotspot formation at microstructural resolution.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a combined experimental-computational analysis of grain-resolved plastic deformation during rapid cyclic loading, linking measured 3DXRD data with thermomechanical simulations.
watch_item: The approach is promising but depends on accurate microstructure representation and loading conditions, so hotspot predictions may be sensitive to modeling assumptions.
paper_title: Subgrain-resolved Analysis of Degradation in Cu Metallization via Scanning 3DXRD and Thermomechanical Modeling
